Title: Oilseed-based metabolic engineering of astaxanthin and related ketocarotenoids using a plant-derived pathway: Lab-to-field-to-application

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in developing pASTA camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-carotene ketolation/hydroxylation route to astaxanthin (Adonis plant-derived pathway)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: β-carotene-derived hydroxylated ketocarotenoid intermediate(s), especially adonirubin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes β-ring 4-dehydrogenation/oxidation in the Adonis pathway, including efficient conversion of adonirubin toward astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and converts late intermediates to astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural red pigment with strong antioxidant activity; valuable for aquaculture feed and food applications
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transiently infiltrated leaves; camelina seeds/seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high astaxanthin accumulation and reduces buildup of ketocarotenoid intermediates relative to bacterial pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with carotenoid profiling by TLC/HPLC-DAD; stable seed-specific transgenic expression in camelina with RT-PCR and metabolite profiling; field evaluation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration in leaves; seed-specific expression in greenhouse and multi-season field conditions in US and UK
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter in N. benthamiana transient assays; seed-specific glycinin promoter in camelina
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In pASTA lines producing ketocarotenoids, smaller seeds, reduced seed oil content, delayed germination, reduced tocopherol, early seedling chlorosis/bleaching
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value gene for oilseed engineering of natural astaxanthin; preferable to bacterial crtW/crtZ in plant hosts and suitable for stacking with precursor-supply genes such as PSY
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the Adonis-derived ASTA module; ASTA alone performed similarly to COMBI and better than bacterial crtWZ for astaxanthin purity in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBFD2 from Adonis aestivalis was a core component of the plant-derived astaxanthin pathway tested in leaves and camelina seeds. 2. In combination with HBFD1, it yielded a higher proportion of astaxanthin and fewer intermediates than bacterial crtW/crtZ in N. benthamiana. 3. In camelina seeds with ZmPSY, it supported near-complete conversion of β-carotene to ketocarotenoids, with astaxanthin as the major free ketocarotenoid source species product.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in developing pASTA camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-carotene ketolation/hydroxylation route to astaxanthin (Adonis plant-derived pathway)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: β-carotene-derived intermediates including hydroxylated β-ring substrates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Astaxanthin; ketocarotenoid intermediates such as adonirubin/adonixanthin in pathway sequence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation/dehydrogenation of 4-hydroxy-β-ionone ring-containing carotenoid intermediates in the Adonis astaxanthin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly participates in target-product formation through late pathway conversions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway component, but not individually resolved as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural antioxidant red pigment for aquaculture coloration and food applications
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Transiently infiltrated leaves; camelina seeds/seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with CBFD2 enables efficient astaxanthin production with fewer intermediates than bacterial pathway in plant host
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with TLC/HPLC-DAD; stable camelina transgenics with RT-PCR and carotenoid profiling under greenhouse and field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium leaf infiltration; seed-specific expression in greenhouse and field-grown camelina
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter in transient assays; seed-specific glycinin promoter in camelina
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of pASTA module associated with reduced seed size, lower oil content, slower germination, reduced tocopherol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful plant-derived ketocarotenoid pathway gene for engineering astaxanthin-rich oilseeds with improved product purity over bacterial routes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assessed only in combination with CBFD2 as the ASTA module; individual contribution not dissected separately in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HBFD1 is one of the two Adonis core pathway enzymes used to build the plant-derived astaxanthin pathway. 2. Co-expression with CBFD2 outperformed bacterial crtW/crtZ in generating astaxanthin in N. benthamiana. 3. With CBFD2 and ZmPSY in camelina seeds, it enabled substantial ketocarotenoid accumulation dominated by astaxanthin and related intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing pASTA camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step from GGPP into carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of GGPP to form phytoene, increasing flux into carotenoid biosynthesis and β-carotene supply
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for β-carotene and downstream ketocarotenoid/astaxanthin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural antioxidant red pigment for aquaculture and food applications
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances precursor flux for β-carotene biosynthesis, supporting high ketocarotenoid accumulation in camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in camelina with RT-PCR confirmation and seed carotenoid profiling in greenhouse and field trials
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in greenhouse and multi-site field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greater carotenoid flux associated with reduced tocopherol and oil content in engineered seeds due to shared GGPP precursor demand
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply gene to stack with downstream ketocarotenoid enzymes for improving astaxanthin yield in oilseeds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because camelina seeds naturally contain relatively low β-carotene, necessitating increased pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPSY was introduced to boost carotenoid precursor supply in camelina seeds. 2. Its co-expression with CBFD2 and HBFD1 enabled near-complete conversion of β-carotene into ketocarotenoids. 3. The resulting pASTA seeds accumulated about 136 µg/g DW ketocarotenoids, including about 47 µg/g DW astaxanthin under reported conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves as TP-crtW
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces keto groups onto β-ionone rings of carotenoids in the bacterial astaxanthin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms ketocarotenoid intermediates leading to astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; pathway less efficient than Adonis route in plant host
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ketocarotenoid formation but leads to more intermediate accumulation and lower astaxanthin proportion than Adonis pathway in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana followed by TLC/HPLC-DAD metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accumulation of intermediates such as adonirubin and canthaxanthin indicates incomplete conversion in plant host
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less preferred than plant-derived enzymes for astaxanthin engineering in plants under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Plastid-targeted using Arabidopsis Rubisco small subunit transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtW was tested as a bacterial ketolase benchmark in transient leaf assays. 2. Together with crtZ, it generated ketocarotenoids but with more intermediate buildup than the Adonis pathway. 3. The bacterial route produced a lower astaxanthin fraction than ASTA in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in infiltrated leaves as TP-crtZ
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene-derived ketocarotenoid intermediates toward astaxanthin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Catalyzes late conversion to target product but incompletely in plant host
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; low efficiency of adonirubin-to-astaxanthin conversion suggested as bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to astaxanthin production but inefficiently converts adonirubin, causing intermediate accumulation in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with TLC/HPLC-DAD carotenoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete conversion causes lower astaxanthin purity and higher adonirubin/canthaxanthin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparator enzyme but inferior to Adonis pathway for plant engineering in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper specifically suggests low efficiency of crtZ-mediated adonirubin to astaxanthin conversion in plant host
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. crtZ was evaluated as part of the bacterial astaxanthin pathway in N. benthamiana. 2. The study indicates that crtZ poorly converted adonirubin into astaxanthin in the plant host. 3. This inefficiency likely explains the higher accumulation of bacterial-pathway intermediates relative to the Adonis pathway.
